label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Since February usually has 28 days, except in leap years when it has 29. Wait, but the question says the "last day of February," so if 2001 is a leap year, it would be February 29th, otherwise February 28th. Let me check if 2001 is a leap year. Leap years are divisible by 4, but if they're divisible by 100, they must also be divisible by 400. 2001 divided by 4 is 500.25, so it's not a leap year. So Jane's birthday is February 28, 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So that means today's date is February 28, 2017 (since 2001 + 16 = 2017). Now we need to find the date a month ago from today. So a month before February 28, 2017, would be January 28, 2017. Let me confirm: if today is February 28, then subtracting one month would take us to January 28. Because each month has different numbers of days, but since February 28 is the 28th day of February, the same day of the month in January would be January 28. Looking at the options, option D is 01/28/2017. Let me check the other options just to be sure. Option A is November 12, 2016, which doesn't make sense because we're looking for January 2017. Option B is January 21, 2017, which is earlier in the month. Option C is January 14, 2017, even earlier. Option E is February 3, 2017, which is still in February. So the correct answer should be D.
